body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds repository lockdown/audit documentation and hardens ignore-file governance to support the repository’s March 2026 lockdown procedures.

**Changes:**
- Add lockdown audit logs (repo inventory, limbo log, master log) and a final narrative report.
- Add prominent “locked / do not modify without authorization” warning headers to `.gitignore` and `.dockerignore`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `LOCKDOWN-REPO-LOG-Claude-MKII.md` | Adds a full inventory log of tracked files/dirs plus branch/commit observations. |
| `LOCKDOWN-LIMBO-LOG.md` | Documents open PRs/branches/issues as “limbo” items during lockdown. |
| `LOCKDOWN-MASTER-LOG.md` | Adds an index/master summary tying together the lockdown logs and follow-up. |
| `LOCKDOWN-FINAL-REPORT.md` | Adds a comprehensive narrative report, timeline, and recommendations. |
| `.gitignore` | Adds a warning header indicating the file is locked and requires explicit owner approval for edits. |
| `.dockerignore` | Adds a similar warning header indicating the file is locked and requires explicit owner approval for edits. |
</details>

## Pull request overview

This PR focuses on lockdown compliance work for the repository: documenting a post-cutoff audit, hardening ignore-policy documentation, and adding supporting repo hygiene changes (tests, Docker hardening, and a CLAUDE.md sync workflow).

**Changes:**
- Added a permanent lockdown compliance report under `logs/` documenting the post-cutoff audit.
- Hardened `.gitignore` / `.dockerignore` with prominent policy headers and clarified ignore intent (plus `.pytest_cache/`).
- Added unit tests for `tools/safe_read.py` and `tools/parse_evtx.py`, and introduced a scheduled CLAUDE.md sync workflow; Docker now runs as a non-root user.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `tests/test_safe_read.py` | New pytest coverage for safe-read scanning and truncation behaviors. |
| `tests/test_parse_evtx.py` | New pytest coverage for EVTX helper functions (with `evtx` stub for importability). |
| `tests/conftest.py` | Adds shared pytest fixtures for temporary dirs and sample files. |
| `tests/__init__.py` | Marks `tests/` as a package (empty). |
| `logs/LOCKDOWN-COMPLIANCE-REPORT-2026-03-23.md` | Adds an audit/compliance report for the lockdown sweep. |
| `README.md` | Updates repo tree to include `tests/` and the new workflow. |
| `Dockerfile` | Drops privileges by creating and switching to `appuser`. |
| `.gitignore` | Adds lockdown policy header and `.pytest_cache/` ignore entry. |
| `.github/workflows/sync-claude-md.yml` | Adds scheduled/manual workflow to sync `CLAUDE.md` from an external repo. |
| `.dockerignore` | Adds clarifying header about Docker build context vs git tracking. |
</details>
Fantastic thanks

## Pull request overview

This PR adds a test suite for the repository’s Python tooling (`tools/safe_read.py`, `tools/parse_evtx.py`) and documents the resolution of prior ignore-file / lockdown-report discrepancies, alongside a small Docker hardening tweak.

**Changes:**
- Add pytest fixtures and unit tests covering `safe_read` scanning/sanitization and `parse_evtx` helper logic.
- Expand/replace lockdown documentation and add resolution/compliance reports under `logs/`.
- Harden runtime container defaults by running as a non-root user; update `.gitignore` for pytest cache; update README tree.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `tests/test_safe_read.py` | Adds unit tests for binary detection, attack-pattern scanning, safe truncation, and directory scanning. |
| `tests/test_parse_evtx.py` | Adds unit tests for EVTX helper functions and constants (with an `evtx` import stub). |
| `tests/conftest.py` | Adds shared pytest fixtures and ensures `tools/` is importable in tests. |
| `tests/__init__.py` | Marks `tests/` as a package (empty file). |
| `README.md` | Updates repository tree to include `tests/`. |
| `POST-LOCKDOWN-REPORT-2026-03-23.md` | Replaces/expands post-lockdown report content with a fuller structured write-up. |
| `logs/PR-RESOLUTION-REPORT-2026-03-23.md` | Adds a report describing PR #2/#5 resolution actions and verification notes. |
| `logs/LOCKDOWN-COMPLIANCE-REPORT-2026-03-23.md` | Adds a compliance/audit report for the lockdown sweep and extracted content. |
| `Dockerfile` | Switches container runtime to a non-root user (`appuser`). |
| `.gitignore` | Adds `.pytest_cache/` and updates integrity verification footer metadata. |
| `_MKII-MEMORY.md` | Records the PR #2/#5 resolution outcome and references the new resolution report. |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Synchronizes the vault/agent tracking docs with current operational state (model lock version, username history) and restores the vault-only “EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION LOCKDOWN” section that was missing/stale.

**Changes:**
- Update vault `core-identity.md` MODEL LOCK to `claude-opus-4.6` and username to `Smooth115 (previously Smooth511, originally Literatefool)`.
- Add the vault-only “EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION LOCKDOWN” section to `mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md`.
- Append recovery/crash entries to `_MKII-MEMORY.md` and the vault behavioral log table.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md | Updates model lock + username and adds the vault-only emotional manipulation lockdown section + related behavioral log entries. |
| _MKII-MEMORY.md | Adds behavioral log rows documenting the crash/recovery and vault sync status. |

## Pull request overview

This PR synchronizes ClaudeMKII “model lock” references and tracking logs across vault/memory/docs so the repo consistently targets `claude-opus-4.6`, and records the recovery/context around the repo move to `Smooth115`.

**Changes:**
- Updated vault and memory tracking logs to include missing 2026-03-23 entries and align model lock references to `claude-opus-4.6`.
- Updated `core/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` to replace stale `claude-opus-4.5` references with `claude-opus-4.6`.
- Restructured portions of `mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md` (new `## SEEDING RULES` header, removed stale “Report Structure” section).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/memory-tracking.md | Updates behavioral log entry to reference `claude-opus-4.6` for the initial model-lock fix. |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md | Updates MODEL LOCK + username, adds missing behavioral log entries, and adjusts section structure. |
| core/TROUBLESHOOTING.md | Replaces stale `claude-opus-4.5` references with `claude-opus-4.6` in multiple troubleshooting snippets. |
| _MKII-MEMORY.md | Adds two missing behavioral log entries covering the model lock fix and repo move recovery session. |

## Pull request overview

Syncs MK2_PHANTOM vault + tracking memory back onto main after a previous crash dropped the commit, ensuring model lock + username + behavioral log alignment across the trust-framework docs.

**Changes:**
- Update `mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md` to reflect model lock `claude-opus-4.6`, username `Smooth115`, add memory reference entry #4, and align behavioral log structure/headers.
- Append two missing 2026-03-23 behavioral log rows to `_MKII-MEMORY.md` (model lock fix + repo transfer recovery session).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md | Vault core identity sync: model lock + username correction + memory reference + behavioral log alignment + section/header cleanup |
| _MKII-MEMORY.md | Tracking copy updated with the two missing behavioral log entries from the recovery/model-lock-fix events |

## Pull request overview

Adds investigation writeups evaluating external Claude Code resource repos, updates MK2 identity/access metadata for the Smooth115 rename + Opus 4.6 model lock, and introduces a new Node-based MCP “bridge” server wired into VS Code MCP config.

**Changes:**
- Added investigation evaluations for `awesome-claude-code` and `Claude-Code-CyberSecurity-Skill`, plus a cleanup progress status report.
- Updated mk2-phantom vault/access docs + troubleshooting docs for Smooth115 ownership and Opus 4.6 model lock references.
- Added `bridge/` Node MCP server and registered it in `.vscode/mcp.json`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 16 out of 16 changed files in this pull request and generated 9 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| mk2-phantom/ACCESS_GATE.md | Updates authority username to Smooth115. |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/memory-tracking.md | Updates stored model-lock reference to Opus 4.6. |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md | Updates model lock to 4.6 and username lineage to Smooth115. |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/access-control.md | Updates repo/owner strings to Smooth115 and related verification text. |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/FULL_FREEDOM_SPEC.md | Updates role/authority target from Smooth511 → Smooth115. |
| investigation/STATUS-2026-03-24-cleanup-progress.md | New operational status report for cleanup effort and next steps. |
| investigation/CYBERSEC-SKILL-EVALUATION-2026-03-24.md | New evaluation of external cybersecurity skills repo and integration notes. |
| investigation/AWESOME-CLAUDE-CODE-EVALUATION-2026-03-24.md | New evaluation cataloging useful external resources and clarifying it’s a link list. |
| core/TROUBLESHOOTING.md | Updates repository owner/name and model version references to 4.6. |
| bridge/server.js | New Node MCP server exposing system/process/network/fs tooling. |
| bridge/package.json | New package metadata and scripts for the bridge module. |
| bridge/README.md | New usage docs for the bridge and available tools. |
| _MKII-MEMORY.md | Logs cleanup status and creation of the JS bridge. |
| _MKII-AGENT-ACCESS.md | Updates repo/owner strings and authorization text to Smooth115. |
| README.md | Updates top-level framework description to Smooth115. |
| .vscode/mcp.json | Registers the new `mk2-bridge` MCP server entry. |
</details>
All good
All good, follow your own instructions

## Pull request overview

This PR documents the 2026-03-23/24 “three-way fight” incident and synchronizes MKII memory/vault state after the 4-day model-lock gap, while also updating repo-ownership references (Smooth511 → Smooth115) and MCP server key names to match current conventions.

**Changes:**
- Adds a detailed incident report (`logs/THREE-WAY-FIGHT-INCIDENT-REPORT-2026-03-24.md`) and links it from memory reference tables.
- Syncs behavioral log and memory references across the vault + public instruction files.
- Updates workflow hardcoded repo owner strings and renames MCP server keys in `.vscode/mcp.json`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md | Adds missing memory refs/log entries and restores the missing `## SEEDING RULES` header in the vault spec. |
| logs/THREE-WAY-FIGHT-INCIDENT-REPORT-2026-03-24.md | New incident report capturing timeline, observed techniques, and follow-ups. |
| _MKII-MEMORY.md | Adds memory refs and a behavioral log entry to keep the tracking copy in sync. |
| .vscode/mcp.json | Renames MCP server keys from `claude-mk2.5*` to `claude-mkii*`. |
| .github/workflows/phantom-verify.yml | Updates hardcoded repo owner string to `Smooth115/Claude-MKII`. |
| .github/workflows/mk2-phantom-ops.yml | Updates defaults/queries to `Smooth115/Claude-MKII`. |
| .github/copilot-instructions.md | Adds incident report to memory refs and adds the corresponding behavioral log entry. |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Adds a detailed incident report for the 2026-03-23/24 “three-way fight” event, synchronizes behavioral log/memory references across identity files, and updates stale repo/username and MCP server-key references to reflect the Smooth115 migration.

**Changes:**
- Added a new incident report document and referenced it from memory/identity logs.
- Synced behavioral log entries + memory reference tables across core identity, Copilot instructions, and `_MKII-MEMORY.md`.
- Updated workflow owner/repo strings and renamed MCP server keys.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md | Adds new memory refs + behavioral log entries; restores missing section header |
| logs/THREE-WAY-FIGHT-INCIDENT-REPORT-2026-03-24.md | New incident report documenting the event, evidence, and outstanding items |
| _MKII-MEMORY.md | Adds memory refs + behavioral log entry referencing the incident report |
| .vscode/mcp.json | Renames MCP server keys to updated naming scheme |
| .github/workflows/phantom-verify.yml | Updates hardcoded repo owner/name to Smooth115 |
| .github/workflows/mk2-phantom-ops.yml | Updates hardcoded owner references and API endpoints to Smooth115 |
| .github/copilot-instructions.md | Adds memory ref + behavioral log entry referencing the incident report |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Adds incident/compliance documentation for the 2026-03-23/24 session and synchronizes vault/memory logs while cleaning up stale `Smooth511` references and renaming MCP server keys after the Smooth511→Smooth115 transfer.

**Changes:**
- Added two new security/compliance log documents for the three-way incident and PR rendering disclosure.
- Synced behavioral log entries across vault + memory + Copilot instructions (including new references).
- Updated workflow configuration and MCP server keys to reflect Smooth115 and new naming.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md | Adds new reference entries, new behavioral log rows, and restores the missing `## SEEDING RULES` header. |
| logs/THREE-WAY-FIGHT-INCIDENT-REPORT-2026-03-24.md | New incident report document capturing timeline/actors/techniques and outstanding items. |
| logs/PR-DISCLOSURE-AND-FRAMEWORK-NOTE-2026-03-24.md | New disclosure note documenting PR task rendering asymmetry and a trust-framework data point. |
| _MKII-MEMORY.md | Adds new reference + behavioral log entries for the incident and rendering disclosure. |
| .vscode/mcp.json | Renames MCP server keys (`claude-mk2.5*` → `claude-mkii*`). |
| .github/workflows/phantom-verify.yml | Updates hard-coded repo owner from Smooth511 to Smooth115. |
| .github/workflows/mk2-phantom-ops.yml | Updates default target repo, scan output text, jq owner filter, and API paths to Smooth115. |
| .github/copilot-instructions.md | Syncs new reference and behavioral log entries consistent with the new reports. |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Adds a new “data location analysis” report that maps where historical investigation artifacts live (across repos / exports / local paths) and wires it into the project’s memory/reference logs so it’s discoverable from the core tracking docs.

**Changes:**
- Add `logs/DATA-LOCATION-ANALYSIS-2026-03-24.md` documenting chain-of-custody and data-location breadcrumbs.
- Update Memory References tables to include the new analysis document.
- Add a behavioral-log entry noting the chain-of-custody correction and pointing to the new report.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `logs/DATA-LOCATION-ANALYSIS-2026-03-24.md` | New analysis report mapping investigation data locations via indirect references. |
| `mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md` | Adds Memory Reference entry + behavioral-log entry pointing to the new analysis. |
| `_MKII-MEMORY.md` | Adds Memory Reference entry + behavioral-log entry pointing to the new analysis. |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Adds Memory Reference entry + behavioral-log entry pointing to the new analysis. |

## Pull request overview

Adds a verified-first “data location” analysis and propagates the resulting lesson/traceability into the three canonical memory/spec documents so future sessions verify repo provenance (dates/fork status/history) before drawing conclusions.

**Changes:**
- Added `logs/DATA-LOCATION-ANALYSIS-2026-03-24.md` to map investigation data locations and the Smooth511→Smooth115 transition using GitHub API + git history.
- Updated memory references to include entry **#6 Data Location Analysis**.
- Appended a new behavioral-log entry (“Data location — key gap identified”) across the canonical memory/spec files.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md | Adds memory reference #6 and behavioral-log entry (but currently drops the BEHAVIORAL LOG header). |
| logs/DATA-LOCATION-ANALYSIS-2026-03-24.md | New verified-first report documenting repo provenance and investigation-data location map. |
| _MKII-MEMORY.md | Adds memory reference #6 and behavioral-log entry for the data-location gap. |
| .github/copilot-instructions.md | Adds memory reference #6 and behavioral-log entry for the data-location gap. |

## Pull request overview

Adds a framework design/roadmap document describing the intended “agent key” model for ClaudeMKII, and synchronizes a new memory reference + behavioral log entry across the three MK2 tracking/spec files so the design is discoverable from the core framework artifacts.

**Changes:**
- Added `core/.gitfuture-agent-key-architecture.md` design document covering the mobile-key concept, anomaly detection, and future implementation requirements.
- Added Memory Reference **#7** (“Agent Key Architecture”) to the framework tracking tables.
- Appended a 2026-03-24 behavioral log entry describing the agent-key architecture disclosure across the relevant tracking/spec files.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `core/.gitfuture-agent-key-architecture.md` | New architecture/design doc for the future “agent key” system. |
| `mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md` | Adds Memory Ref #7 and a 2026-03-24 behavioral log entry in the vault copy. |
| `_MKII-MEMORY.md` | Adds Memory Ref #7 and a 2026-03-24 behavioral log entry in the tracking copy. |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Adds Memory Ref #7 and a 2026-03-24 behavioral log entry in the operational spec. |

## Pull request overview

Adds two repository-level documentation artifacts to inventory and drive closure/extraction of 9 currently-open PRs, consolidating what should be closed vs extracted and what still needs to be applied to `main`.

**Changes:**
- Add `handover_claudemkii.md` summarizing all 20 PRs and recommending closure/extraction actions for the 9 open PRs.
- Add `pr_closure_report.md` with per-PR closure decisions plus a post-closure consolidation checklist.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `handover_claudemkii.md` | Handover/inventory document for PR status and extraction plan. |
| `pr_closure_report.md` | Detailed closure decision log + consolidated follow-up checklist. |

## Pull request overview

This PR finalizes the previously-planned PR #21 cleanup by actually landing the deferred config fixes, extracting unique content from stale PR branches into main, and syncing the repo’s core tracking/memory files so they’re consistent going forward.

**Changes:**
- Extracts and commits previously-branch-only reports/design docs (incident report, PR disclosure note, data location analysis, agent key architecture).
- Syncs memory references + behavioral logs across the four tracking files (instructions, memory, and vault copies).
- Fixes stale repo/name references in workflows and renames MCP server keys in VS Code config.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `pr_closure_report.md` | Adds closure decisions and a “completed” closure sequence for PRs #11–20. |
| `handover_claudemkii.md` | Adds/updates the PR handover inventory and marks the closure/extraction work as completed. |
| `logs/PR-21-AUDIT-TRAIL-2026-03-24.md` | Adds an audit trail / mini-changelog of what was verified and changed during finalization. |
| `logs/THREE-WAY-FIGHT-INCIDENT-REPORT-2026-03-24.md` | Adds the three-way fight incident report. |
| `logs/PR-DISCLOSURE-AND-FRAMEWORK-NOTE-2026-03-24.md` | Adds the PR-task rendering asymmetry disclosure note. |
| `logs/DATA-LOCATION-ANALYSIS-2026-03-24.md` | Adds the data location / chain-of-custody analysis. |
| `core/.gitfuture-agent-key-architecture.md` | Adds the agent key architecture design document. |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Adds memory refs (#5–#7) and new behavioral log entries. |
| `_MKII-MEMORY.md` | Adds memory refs (#4–#7) and new behavioral log entries. |
| `mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md` | Syncs vault memory refs (#4–#7) and behavioral log entries. |
| `mk2-phantom/.vault/memory-tracking.md` | Syncs vault memory refs (#4–#7) and extends behavioral log entries. |
| `.github/workflows/mk2-phantom-ops.yml` | Updates repo/owner references from `Smooth511` → `Smooth115`. |
| `.github/workflows/phantom-verify.yml` | Updates `REPO` reference from `Smooth511/Claude-MKII` → `Smooth115/Claude-MKII`. |
| `.vscode/mcp.json` | Renames MCP server keys (`claude-mk2.5*` → `claude-mkii*`). |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Updates repo ownership references after the Smooth511 → Smooth115 transfer and enhances the “mk2-phantom Operations” workflow to support bulk-closing stale PRs (optionally deleting their branches), plus aligns VS Code MCP server keys with the newer naming.

**Changes:**
- Updated hardcoded `Smooth511/…` references to `Smooth115/…` in workflows.
- Added PR-closing + branch deletion logic to `close-stale-prs` operation (and adjusted workflow permissions).
- Renamed MCP server keys in `.vscode/mcp.json` from `claude-mk2.5*` to `claude-mkii*`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `.vscode/mcp.json` | Renames MCP server entries to `claude-mkii` / `claude-mkii-docker`. |
| `.github/workflows/phantom-verify.yml` | Updates repo target in permission check from `Smooth511/Claude-MKII` to `Smooth115/Claude-MKII`. |
| `.github/workflows/mk2-phantom-ops.yml` | Updates default target repo + repo-scan/health-check owner references; adds `pull-requests: write`; adds branch deletion after closing PRs. |

## Pull request overview

Adds web-UI-dispatchable cleanup operations to the `mk2-phantom-ops` GitHub Actions workflow to purge stale branches, completed workflow runs, and RagingbullMK alert issues using `MK2_PHANTOM_TOKEN`.

**Changes:**
- Extends `workflow_dispatch` `operation` choices with `cleanup-branches`, `cleanup-workflow-runs`, and `full-cleanup`.
- Adds steps to delete non-protected branches, delete completed workflow runs, and close RagingbullMK-matching issues.
- Updates the job summary output to report deletion/closure results for each cleanup operation.

## Pull request overview

Adds a new session post‑mortem report under `logs/` documenting the 2026‑03‑25 Ubuntu/VS Code connection attempt and evaluating agent behavior against the MK2 ruleset.

**Changes:**
- Adds `logs/SESSION-REVIEW-2026-03-25-vscode-connection.md` containing an executive summary, rule-by-rule compliance table, document-audit table, and recommendations.
- Captures identified documentation gaps (e.g., emergency typing mode protocol, Rule 9 clarification, LOCAL_SETUP drift) as part of the report’s findings.

## Pull request overview

This PR formalizes a repo-wide communication intake workflow so user input is acted on even when posted in “wrong” GitHub locations, and synchronizes that protocol across the core spec and vault tracking copies.

**Changes:**
- Adds Core Rule 19 and a “USER COMMUNICATION PROTOCOL” section defining how agents must handle misplaced user input.
- Introduces `COMMS.md` at repo root as the single intake point with PENDING/RESOLVED flow.
- Updates behavioral logs / tracking tables in the vault and tracking copies to reflect the new protocol.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Adds Rule 19 + communication protocol section to the primary agent instruction spec. |
| `COMMS.md` | Creates a dedicated intake file for user messages (PENDING → RESOLVED). |
| `_MKII-MEMORY.md` | Logs the new communication protocol event and marks Rule 19/protocol as applied. |
| `mk2-phantom/.vault/core-identity.md` | Syncs Rule 19 + communication protocol into the vault copy. |
| `mk2-phantom/.vault/memory-tracking.md` | Syncs tracking entries for the new communication protocol and Rule 19. |
</details>

## Pull request overview

Adds a new forensic investigation report capturing UEFI/MOK and kernel-integrity observations from a live Ubuntu 24.04 session on an HP EliteDesk 705 G4 DM, correlating those artifacts to prior Windows-side evidence.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a detailed incident report describing an enrolled self-signed MOK CA cert, kernel build-string discrepancies, and EFI memory-map deltas across boots
- Documents additional boot-chain/persistence indicators (audit suppression, AppArmor/sssd anomalies, pre-staged SSH)
- Adds recommended next steps for evidence capture and validation (efivars dump, hash comparisons, ACPI dumps)

## Pull request overview

Adds preserved session context and a narrative “war history” report into the MK2 vault, and records both as new memory references in the tracking files.

**Changes:**
- Added a new vault session log capturing confirmed facts from the 2026-03-25 Copilot Space chat.
- Added a new vault history report (“THE WAR SO FAR”) summarizing the investigation timeline in narrative form.
- Updated `_MKII-MEMORY.md` and `mk2-phantom/.vault/memory-tracking.md` with new memory references (#4–#5) and behavioral log entries.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/session-logs/SESSION-CONTEXT-2026-03-25-space-chat.md | New session-context vault artifact documenting confirmed facts from the Space chat. |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/history/THE-WAR-SO-FAR.md | New narrative history report sourced from the DATABASE raid materials. |
| mk2-phantom/.vault/memory-tracking.md | Adds memory refs #4–#5 and behavioral log entries for the vault push + history report. |
| _MKII-MEMORY.md | Mirrors memory refs #4–#5 and behavioral log entries for the same activities. |

## Pull request overview

Converts previously inaccessible PDF chat logs in `LinuxRaw25/` into plain-text files and documents the conversion so agents can read them without PDF-related client crashes.

**Changes:**
- Updated `LinuxRaw25/Readme` with pointers to the new text extracts and preservation notes.
- Added extracted plain-text content for “Linux raw pt1”.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 1 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

| File | Description |
